name: NFPA 101 Life Safety Code 2021
copyright_notice: NFPA 101® Life Safety Code® 2021 © 2020 National Fire Protection Association. Section numbers and numeric requirements are referenced as facts; no normative text is reproduced without paraphrase.

sprinklered.label: Sprinklered (NFPA 13)
sprinklered.description: Sprinkler protection significantly extends NFPA 101 travel-distance, dead-end, and common-path limits.

rules.label: Occupant Load Determination
rules.citation: NFPA 101 2021 §7.3.1.2 – Occupant load shall be determined using Table 7.3.1.2 load factors (sq ft per person). Use the load factor that gives the highest occupant load for each space.
rules.remediation: Recalculate occupant load per NFPA 101 Table 7.3.1.2. Verify each space's occupancy classification and recalculate using the appropriate net or gross area floor load factor.

rules.label: Maximum Travel Distance to Exit
rules.citation: NFPA 101 2021 §7.6.1 – Travel distance from any point in the floor to the nearest exit shall not exceed the distances in Table 7.6. Sprinklered buildings have increased travel distance limits.
rules.remediation: Verify travel distance from the most remote point in each occupied space to the nearest exit does not exceed the NFPA 101 limit. Add exits or redistribute occupancy to bring all areas within limit.

rules.label: Egress Capacity Width
rules.citation: NFPA 101 2021 §7.3.3.1 – Egress capacity: stairs 0.3 in/person; level components 0.2 in/person. Minimum door width 32 in., minimum corridor 36 in. (some occupancies require 44 in.).
rules.remediation: Calculate required egress width: occupant load × width factor. Verify each egress component meets the calculated minimum width and the absolute minimums.

rules.label: Exit Discharge Width
rules.citation: NFPA 101 2021 §7.7 – Exit discharge shall lead to a public way. Not less than 50% of exit enclosures shall discharge directly outside at grade.
rules.remediation: Verify that at least 50% of exit stair enclosures discharge directly to grade level exterior without passing through building interior. Redesign exit paths where needed.

rules.label: Emergency Lighting
rules.citation: NFPA 101 2021 §7.9.2 – Emergency lighting: initial ≥ 1 fc average along egress path; at end of 1.5-hr period, average ≥ 0.6 fc. Maximum ratio of high to low illumination: 40:1.
rules.remediation: Install emergency lighting units along all means of egress. Verify battery backup provides 1.5 hr operation. Space fixtures to meet 0.6 fc average at 1.5 hours.

rules.label: Exit Marking and Signage
rules.citation: NFPA 101 2021 §7.10.1 – Exit signs shall be illuminated (internally or externally) and visible from any direction. Letter height ≥ 6 in. Maintain visibility ≥ 100 ft. Battery backup: 1.5 hr.
rules.remediation: Install illuminated exit signs with letters ≥ 6 in. high at each exit and at every change of direction in the exit access path. Provide battery backup to maintain visibility for 1.5 hr.

rules.label: Smoke Compartmentation
rules.citation: NFPA 101 2021 §8.3 – Health care facilities shall be subdivided into smoke compartments not exceeding 22,500 sq ft in area with smoke barriers having a fire-resistance rating of ≥ 1 hr.
rules.remediation: Subdivide the floor into smoke compartments not exceeding 22,500 sq ft each with 1-hr fire-rated smoke barriers. Ensure smoke barriers extend to the roof deck.

rules.label: Fire Alarm System
rules.citation: NFPA 101 2021 §9.6.1 – Fire alarm systems shall be installed in occupancies where required by the applicable occupancy chapter. Generally required in buildings >300 persons or >12,000 sq ft.
rules.remediation: Install an NFPA 72-compliant fire alarm and detection system. Include smoke detection, heat detection, manual pull stations at exits, and audible/visual notification devices throughout.

rules.label: Number & Arrangement of Means of Egress
rules.notes_template: Verify two means of egress where required, located remote from one another (≥ 1/2 of overall diagonal in unsprinklered, 1/3 in sprinklered, per §7.5.1.3.2/3) so that loss of one does not block the other.
rules.citation: NFPA 101 2021 §7.5.1 – Means of egress shall be arranged so that two are remote from each other; the minimum separation is one-half (or one-third for sprinklered buildings) of the maximum overall diagonal dimension of the building or area served.
rules.remediation: Recheck stair/exit placement: separation must equal or exceed the diagonal-rule distance. Add or relocate exits so two remote MOE are provided.

rules.label: Dead-End Corridors
rules.notes_template: Verify dead-end corridor length ≤ {{params.max_dead_end_ft}} ft (most occupancies). Reference NFPA 101 occupancy chapter for any tighter limits.
rules.citation: NFPA 101 2021 §7.5.4 / occupancy chapters – Dead-end corridor length limits depend on occupancy and sprinkler protection.
rules.remediation: Reconfigure corridors to eliminate dead ends or keep them within the occupancy-specific length limit.

rules.label: Common Path of Travel
rules.notes_template: Verify common path of travel ≤ {{params.max_common_path_ft}} ft (general; consult occupancy chapter for tighter limits).
rules.citation: NFPA 101 2021 §7.6.3 / occupancy chapters – Common path of travel limits depend on occupancy and sprinkler protection.
rules.remediation: Reconfigure layout so the longest common path is within the limit; add a second exit or sprinkler protection if exceeded.

rules.label: Stair Geometry & Headroom
rules.notes_template: Verify new stairs (NFPA 101 Table 7.2.2.2.1.1.A): tread depth 11 in. min, riser 4–7 in., width per occupant load (44 in. min for occupant load > 50), headroom ≥ 6 ft 8 in.
rules.citation: NFPA 101 2021 §7.2.2.2 / Table 7.2.2.2.1.1 – New stairs: 11 in. min tread depth; 4–7 in. riser; 44 in. min width above 50 occupants; uniform within ± 3/16 in.
rules.remediation: Detail stairs to comply with NFPA 101 Table 7.2.2.2.1.1.A; tighter than IBC on uniformity (3/16 in.).

rules.label: Egress Doors – Operation
rules.notes_template: Verify egress doors: side-hinged or pivoted swinging; swing in direction of egress where serving > 50 occupants or H/A occupancies; latch hardware operable with one hand without tight grasping/pinching/twisting; max opening force 5 lbf (interior) / 15 lbf (exterior).
rules.citation: NFPA 101 2021 §7.2.1.4 / §7.2.1.7 – Egress doors shall be side-hinged swinging (with limited exceptions), shall swing in direction of egress travel under the §7.2.1.4.2 conditions, and shall be openable from the egress side with not more than one releasing operation.
rules.remediation: Use swinging hardware with a single releasing operation; verify swing direction and operating force per §7.2.1.

rules.label: Occupancy Chapter Cross-Reference
rules.notes_template: Apply the appropriate NFPA 101 occupancy chapter (12 New Assembly, 13 Existing Assembly, 14 Educational, 18 Health Care, 26 Lodging, 28 Hotels, 36 Mercantile, 38 Business, etc.) for occupancy-specific egress, alarm, and protection rules.
rules.citation: NFPA 101 2021 Chapters 12–43 – Occupancy chapters supersede the general core chapters with use-specific requirements.
rules.remediation: Identify the correct occupancy chapter and apply its egress, fire-protection, and operational requirements.

rules.label: Existing Health Care – Smoke Compartments
rules.notes_template: For existing health-care occupancies, verify smoke compartments per §19.3.7 (≤ 22,500 sq ft) and refuge area ≥ 30 net sq ft per non-ambulatory patient (§19.2.3.5).
rules.citation: NFPA 101 2021 §19.3.7 / §19.2.3.5 – Existing health-care occupancies shall provide smoke compartments and refuge space sized for relocated occupants.
rules.remediation: Re-verify each existing-healthcare smoke compartment area and refuge calculations; document on plans.
